title: euphorbia collina var. spathulata
intro: Euphorbia collina var. spathulata, also known as the Spotted Spurge, is a species of succulent plant in the Euphorbiaceae family. It is a low-growing, spreading plant with small, rounded leaves and produces small, white or greenish-white flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial activities, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and digestive problems. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
